references: Abhay Gupta et al. (2020) Designing Advanced Lithium‐Based Batteries for Low‐Temperature Conditions doi:10.1002/aenm.202001972

evidence_type_annotations.claim: evaluation of lithium-metal, lithium-sulfur, and dual-ion batteries
introduction and evaluation of next-generation battery chemistries for low-temperature applications
unique challenges at low temperatures for the three chemistries
Full text excerpt:

Next‐generation chemistries employing alternative anodes with increased solvent compatibility or altogether different operating mechanisms could present an avenue for overcoming many of the low‐temperature hurdles intrinsic to the lithium‐ion battery.

In this article, a brief overview of the challenges in developing lithium‐ion batteries for low‐temperature use is provided, and then an array of nascent battery chemistries are introduced that may be intrinsically better suited for low‐temperature conditions moving forward.

However, these three chemistries also present their own unique challenges at low temperatures, highlighting the balance between traditional low‐temperature electrolyte design and next‐generation approaches.

The latest research status of direct regeneration of spent lithium–ion batteries was reviewed and summarized in focus. The application examples of direct r...[truncated]
Full text excerpt:
Direct regeneration method has been widely concerned by researchers in the field of battery recycling because of its advantages of in situ regeneration, short process and less pollutant emission.

Efficient stripping of material from the collector to obtain pure cathode material has become a first challenge in recycling, for which we report several pretreatment methods currently available for subsequent regeneration processes.

We review and discuss emphatically the research progress of five direct regeneration methods, including solid-state sintering, hydrothermal, eutectic molten salt, electrochemical and chemical lithiation methods.

It is hoped that this review will give readers a comprehensive and basic understanding of direct regeneration methods for used lithium-ion batteries and promote the industrial application of direct regeneration technology.

evidence_type_annotations.claim: our work has focused on the techno-economic analysis

discussed the recent progress in the development of air-stable, electrochemically stable, and cost-effective NaxTMO2, emphasizing the favorable redox-active cations, working mechanisms, and techno-economics
irreversible phase transformations during cycling, poor air stability, complex charge-compensation mechanisms, and relatively high cost of the full cell compared to LiFePO4-based LIBs
Full text excerpt:
Sodium-ion batteries (NIBs), which possess a similar cell configuration and working mechanism, have already been proven as ideal alternatives for large-scale energy storage systems.

Sodium layered transition metal oxides (NaxTMO2, TM = transition metal/s), such as Mn-based sodium layered oxides, represent an important family of cathode materials with the potential to reduce costs, increase energy density and cycling stability, and improve the safety of NIBs for large-scale ener...[truncated]

Our work has focused on the techno-economic analysis, the degradation mechanism of NaxTMO2 upon cycling and air exposure, and the development of effective strategies to improve their electrochemical performances and air stability.

evidence_type_annotations.claim: Multi-Criteria Decision Analysis (MCDA) framework

a comprehensive review and framework for deploying Integrated Energy Storage Systems (IESSs) to enhance grid efficiency and stability, with a focus on hybrid storage systems and regionally tailored strategies
reliance on static cost projections and evolving regulatory landscapes
Full text excerpt:
This study presents a comprehensive review and framework for deploying Integrated Energy Storage Systems (IESSs) to enhance grid efficiency and stability.

By leveraging a Multi-Criteria Decision Analysis (MCDA) framework, this study synthesizes techno-economic optimization, lifecycle emissions, and policy frameworks to evaluate storage technologies such as lithium-ion batteries, pumped hydro storage, and vanadium flow batteries.

The framework prioritizes hybrid storage systems (e.g., battery–supercapacitor configurations), demonstrating 15% higher grid stability in high-renewable penetration scenarios, and validates findings through global case studies, including the Hornsdale Power Reserve (90–95% round-trip efficiency) an...[truncated]

Despite reliance on static cost projections and evolving regulatory landscapes, the MCDA framework’s dynamic adaptation mechanisms, including sensitivity analysis for carbon taxes (USD 100/ton CO2-eq boosts hydrogen viability by 25%), ensure scalability across diverse grids.

Abstract: This study presents a comparative techno-economic and environmental assessment of three leading stationary energy storage technologies: lithium-ion batteries, lead-acid batteries, and hydrogen systems (electrolyzer–tank–fuel cell). The analysis integrates Life Cycle Assessment (LCA) and Le...[truncated]

The results indicate that lithium-ion batteries achieve the lowest LCOS (120–180 EUR/MWh) and high round-trip efficiency (90–95%), making them optimal for short- and medium-duration storage.

evidence_type_annotations.claim: evaluation of lithium-metal, lithium-sulfur, and dual-ion batteries

unique challenges at low temperatures for the three chemistries
Full text excerpt:

Next‐generation chemistries employing alternative anodes with increased solvent compatibility or altogether different operating mechanisms could present an avenue for overcoming many of the low‐temperature hurdles intrinsic to the lithium‐ion battery.

In this article, a brief overview of the challenges in developing lithium‐ion batteries for low‐temperature use is provided, and then an array of nascent battery chemistries are introduced that may be intrinsically better suited for low‐temperature conditions moving forward.

However, these three chemistries also present their own unique challenges at low temperatures, highlighting the balance between traditional low‐temperature electrolyte design and next‐generation approaches.

dynamics.citation: Md Tasbirul Islam et al. (2022) Lithium-Ion Battery Recycling in the Circular Economy: A Review doi:10.3390/recycling7030033
dynamics.title: Lithium-Ion Battery Recycling in the Circular Economy: A Review
dynamics.excerpt: Some of the issues that require further consideration include a techno-economic assessment of the recycling process, safe reverse logistics, a global EV assessment revealing material recovery potential, and a lifecycle assessment of experiments processes (both in the hydrometallurgical and pyrometallurgical processes).

dynamics.citation: Md Tasbirul Islam et al. (2022) Lithium-Ion Battery Recycling in the Circular Economy: A Review doi:10.3390/recycling7030033
dynamics.title: Lithium-Ion Battery Recycling in the Circular Economy: A Review
dynamics.excerpt: This study presents several future research directions that would be useful for academics and policymakers taking necessary steps such as product design, integrated recycling techniques, intra-industry stakeholder cooperation, business model development, techno-economic analysis, and others towards achieving a circular economy in the LIB value chain.

location_or_scope.title: Layered Oxide Cathodes for Sodium-Ion Batteries: Storage Mechanism, Electrochemistry, and Techno-economics
location_or_scope.excerpt: Sodium layered transition metal oxides (NaxTMO2, TM = transition metal/s), such as Mn-based sodium layered oxides, represent an important family of cathode materials with the potential to reduce costs, increase energy density and cycling stability, and improve the safety of NIBs for large-scale energy storage.

location_or_scope.title: Sodium-Ion Batteries: Materials, Performance, and Application in Engineering Systems
location_or_scope.excerpt: Recent advances in electrode materials (e.g., layered oxides, hard carbon composites, metallic alloys) are greatly improving SIB stability, conductivity, capacity, and cycle life.

dynamics.title: Competitive Rechargeable Zinc Batteries for Energy Storage
dynamics.excerpt: Competitive Rechargeable Zinc Batteries for Energy Storage The continuously increased demand for electrical energy and the associated strong growth in renewable energy necessitate robust, sustainable, and cost‐effective stationary energy storage solutions.

dynamics.citation: Md Tasbirul Islam et al. (2022) Lithium-Ion Battery Recycling in the Circular Economy: A Review doi:10.3390/recycling7030033
dynamics.title: Lithium-Ion Battery Recycling in the Circular Economy: A Review
dynamics.excerpt: Some of the issues that require further consideration include a techno-economic assessment of the recycling process, safe reverse logistics, a global EV assessment revealing material recovery potential, and a lifecycle assessment of experiments processes (both in the hydrometallurgical and pyrometallurgical processes).

dynamics.citation: Md Tasbirul Islam et al. (2022) Lithium-Ion Battery Recycling in the Circular Economy: A Review doi:10.3390/recycling7030033
dynamics.title: Lithium-Ion Battery Recycling in the Circular Economy: A Review
dynamics.excerpt: This study presents several future research directions that would be useful for academics and policymakers taking necessary steps such as product design, integrated recycling techniques, intra-industry stakeholder cooperation, business model development, techno-economic analysis, and others towards achieving a circular economy in the LIB value chain.

reversibility.citation: Md Tasbirul Islam et al. (2022) Lithium-Ion Battery Recycling in the Circular Economy: A Review doi:10.3390/recycling7030033
reversibility.title: Lithium-Ion Battery Recycling in the Circular Economy: A Review
reversibility.excerpt: Some of the issues that require further consideration include a techno-economic assessment of the recycling process, safe reverse logistics, a global EV assessment revealing material recovery potential, and a lifecycle assessment of experiments processes (both in the hydrometallurgical and pyrometallurgical processes).

reversibility.citation: Md Tasbirul Islam et al. (2022) Lithium-Ion Battery Recycling in the Circular Economy: A Review doi:10.3390/recycling7030033
reversibility.title: Lithium-Ion Battery Recycling in the Circular Economy: A Review
reversibility.excerpt: The study showed that publications in the area are increasing exponentially, with many focusing on recycling and recovery-related issues; policy and regulatory affairs received less attention than recycling.

counterfactual.citation: Subin Antony Jose et al. (2026) Sodium-Ion Batteries: Materials, Performance, and Application in Engineering Systems doi:10.3390/batteries12050180
counterfactual.title: Sodium-Ion Batteries: Materials, Performance, and Application in Engineering Systems
counterfactual.excerpt: Despite their lower energy density, SIBs tolerate wider temperature ranges and carry a significantly lower risk of thermal runaway compared to lithium-based systems, making them attractive for industrial, transportation, and large-scale power storage.

identity.citation: Wenhua Zuo et al. (2023) Layered Oxide Cathodes for Sodium-Ion Batteries: Storage Mechanism, Electrochemistry, and Techno-economics doi:10.1021/acs.accounts.2c00690
identity.title: Layered Oxide Cathodes for Sodium-Ion Batteries: Storage Mechanism, Electrochemistry, and Techno-economics
identity.excerpt: Layered Oxide Cathodes for Sodium-Ion Batteries: Storage Mechanism, Electrochemistry, and Techno-economics Conspectus Lithium-ion batteries (LIBs) are ubiquitous in all modern portable electronic devices such as mobile phones and laptops as well as for powering hybrid electric vehicles and other large-scale devices.

identity.citation: Wenhua Zuo et al. (2023) Layered Oxide Cathodes for Sodium-Ion Batteries: Storage Mechanism, Electrochemistry, and Techno-economics doi:10.1021/acs.accounts.2c00690
identity.title: Layered Oxide Cathodes for Sodium-Ion Batteries: Storage Mechanism, Electrochemistry, and Techno-economics
identity.excerpt: Sodium-ion batteries (NIBs), which possess a similar cell configuration and working mechanism, have already been proven as ideal alternatives for large-scale energy storage systems.

identity.citation: Wenhua Zuo et al. (2023) Layered Oxide Cathodes for Sodium-Ion Batteries: Storage Mechanism, Electrochemistry, and Techno-economics doi:10.1021/acs.accounts.2c00690
identity.title: Layered Oxide Cathodes for Sodium-Ion Batteries: Storage Mechanism, Electrochemistry, and Techno-economics
identity.excerpt: Our work has focused on the techno-economic analysis, the degradation mechanism of NaxTMO2 upon cycling and air exposure, and the development of effective strategies to improve their electrochemical performances and air stability.

location_or_scope.citation: Wenhua Zuo et al. (2023) Layered Oxide Cathodes for Sodium-Ion Batteries: Storage Mechanism, Electrochemistry, and Techno-economics doi:10.1021/acs.accounts.2c00690
location_or_scope.title: Layered Oxide Cathodes for Sodium-Ion Batteries: Storage Mechanism, Electrochemistry, and Techno-economics
location_or_scope.excerpt: Layered Oxide Cathodes for Sodium-Ion Batteries: Storage Mechanism, Electrochemistry, and Techno-economics Conspectus Lithium-ion batteries (LIBs) are ubiquitous in all modern portable electronic devices such as mobile phones and laptops as well as for powering hybrid electric vehicles and other large-scale devices.

location_or_scope.citation: Wenhua Zuo et al. (2023) Layered Oxide Cathodes for Sodium-Ion Batteries: Storage Mechanism, Electrochemistry, and Techno-economics doi:10.1021/acs.accounts.2c00690
location_or_scope.title: Layered Oxide Cathodes for Sodium-Ion Batteries: Storage Mechanism, Electrochemistry, and Techno-economics
location_or_scope.excerpt: Sodium layered transition metal oxides (NaxTMO2, TM = transition metal/s), such as Mn-based sodium layered oxides, represent an important family of cathode materials with the potential to reduce costs, increase energy density and cycling stability, and improve the safety of NIBs for large-scale energy storage.

location_or_scope.excerpt: Recent advances in electrode materials (e.g., layered oxides, hard carbon composites, metallic alloys) are greatly improving SIB stability, conductivity, capacity, and cycle life.

dynamics.excerpt: Some of the issues that require further consideration include a techno-economic assessment of the recycling process, safe reverse logistics, a global EV assessment revealing material recovery potential, and a lifecycle assessment of experiments processes (both in the hydrometallurgical and pyrometallurgical processes).
